name: network
context: Inspect, wait for, mock, modify, block, and assert browser network traffic in Playwright tests and automation scripts.
info: Playwright network control lets agents observe requests and responses, wait for specific API calls, stub backend behavior, modify upstream responses, block resource classes, and validate that UI actions produce the expected HTTP traffic. This context covers page and context routes, route.fulfill, route.fetch, route.abort, waitForRequest, waitForResponse, HAR recording concepts, service worker caveats, and safe logging. Agents use it when isolating frontend behavior, reproducing backend errors, speeding up browser research, or debugging why a dynamic page did not load expected data.

sections.title: Mock responses
sections.body: Use `page.route(pattern, handler)` or `context.route(pattern, handler)` to intercept requests. `route.fulfill({ status, headers, contentType, body })` returns a mock response. Register mocks before `page.goto()` if the page loads data during initial render. Scope mocks narrowly so unrelated requests continue to exercise the real stack.

sections.title: Modify upstream responses
sections.body: Use `const response = await route.fetch()` to make the real request, then inspect or mutate the response before calling `route.fulfill({ response, body })`. This is useful for changing one field while preserving realistic headers, status, and most body content.

sections.title: Wait for traffic
sections.body: Create wait promises before the action: `const p = page.waitForResponse(r => r.url().endsWith('/api/me') && r.status() === 200); await page.click(...); await p;`. This avoids missing fast responses. Use predicates when URL globs are too broad or when status/method matters.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.body: Service workers can intercept requests before Playwright routes see them; disable service workers in the context when needed. Do not log authorization headers or cookies in CI. Blocking images and fonts speeds research, but do not use that mode for visual tests. Use [[playwright/api-testing]] for direct API checks that do not need a browser.
